title: How can I use an automation to take someone from a people column and assign them to the item?
excerpt:
I have an approval workflow board and form. Staff fills out the form and enters in 1-3 approval people as “person 1”, “person 2” etc. 
The approvals need to happen sequentially, so I don’t want everyone assigned from the…

posts.author: Kennedy808 (Bill Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have an approval workflow board and form. Staff fills out the form and enters in 1-3 approval people as “person 1”, “person 2” etc.

The approvals need to happen sequentially, so I don’t want everyone assigned from the beginning. It should only be when it’s their turn.

I’d like to have an automation that says “assign person #1 as Next Approver” on item creation. Then “When approval #1 is done, assign person #2 as Next Approver” etc. (Using Assignments so that the task shows in their My Work.)

But the stock automation for assign/re-assign can only choose specific people in my company, not the people column from the form submission.

So for now, I have to hard code the approval chains for each department, setting up the automations with specific people at each step.

Anyone have a cool idea for how to dynamically assign people at each step based on the form submission?

Thanks

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
How do you track ‘Approval #1’ is done currently? ie is it a status/date?

For this, you’ll want to use a workflow instead of a standard automation. This will look different based on how you track approval status. But here’s an example.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Kennedy808 !

A native setup for this is a bit limited today. Monday automations can work with People columns as triggers and conditions, but the built-in assign and reassign actions are generally set up around selected users rather than dynamically taking the value from another People column in the approval chain. The custom automation builder lets you use columns like Person as part of triggers and conditions, but it does not clearly provide a native “copy Person 1 into Next Approver, then Person 2 into Next Approver” style action in the way you’re describing.

Because of that, the cleanest native approach is usually to keep the approvers in separate People columns such as Person 1, Person 2, and Person 3, then drive the process with status changes and notifications instead of trying to fully reassign My Work dynamically. For example, on item creation you can notify Person 1, then when Approval 1 is complete notify Person 2, and so on. That preserves the sequence even if it does not fully solve the dynamic assignment piece.

If having the current approver appear in My Work is the key requirement, that is usually where teams move to an integration or custom workflow layer, since the out of the box automations do not seem built for passing one People column value into another People column step by step. The People column does support AI-based assignment and manual assignment, but that is a different pattern from a sequential approval chain based on form-submitted People fields.

So in practice, I’d frame it like this:
Right now, a fully dynamic native approval handoff from Person 1 to Person 2 to Person 3 does not appear to be straightforward with stock automations alone. A notification-based sequence is usually the closest native option, and if you need true reassignment into a single Next Approver field for My Work, that is more likely to need an integration or custom solution.

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Bill, I’ve run into the same limitation before. The stock automations can’t pull directly from a People column dynamically, so the usual workaround is to use a combination of status changes and a simple integration via Integromat/Make or Zapier. Basically: when the item is created, the integration reads the People column from the form, then assigns the first person. When that approval is marked done, the integration moves to the next person and updates the “Assigned To” field. It adds a tiny bit of setup, but it gives you the dynamic sequential assignment you’re looking for without hardcoding each chain.

posts.author: Kennedy808 (Bill Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the reply. I’ll take a look at those integrations and see if we might want to give one a try.

I guess for now I’ll just stick to the hard coded automations.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Kennedy808

This is a good use case for Make, since native automations can’t dynamically pick people from other columns.

You can build a scenario like this:

  • Trigger: When a new item is created from the form.

  • Make reads the values from Person 1, Person 2, Person 3 columns.

  • It then updates the “Next Approver” column with Person 1.

Then for the approval steps:

  • When Approval 1 status changes to Approved, Make automatically assigns Person 2 to the Next Approver column.

  • When Approval 2 is completed, it assigns Person 3, and so on.

This way the approvers are taken dynamically from the form submission, so you don’t need to hardcode approval chains for each department. It also ensures the approvals happen sequentially and only the current approver gets assigned, so it appears correctly in their My Work.
